patches.summary: Wire pagination.nextCursor and pagination.hasMore paths into list commands.
patches.reason: The OpenAPI parser (parser.go:4295) only walks top-level response properties when scanning for next_cursor / has_more. Tella's REST envelope nests them under {pagination:{...}}, so Pagination.NextCursorPath and HasMoreField were emitted as empty strings and the --all loop exited after page 1.
patches.validated_outcome: videos list --all returns all 232 videos across 16 cursor-followed pages (was 15).

patches.summary: Add unwrapResultsArray, unwrapSingleEnvelope, printQuiet, printPlain helpers; route --csv/--quiet/--plain above the piped JSON gate; unwrap envelopes before --select/--compact.
patches.reason: The generated gate `flags.asJSON || !isTerminal()` short-circuited explicit format flags when stdout was piped (the agent / shell case). printOutputWithFlags also fed CSV/plain renderers an envelope-shaped JSON instead of a flat array. --quiet was silent despite docs saying 'one value per line'. --plain had no renderer at all.
patches.validated_outcome: videos list --csv --select id,name | head -3 emits CSV header + 2 rows; videos list --quiet --select id emits one ID per line; videos list --plain --select id,views emits tab-separated rows; videos/playlists/clips get --select id,name returns the populated {id,name} object.

patches.summary: Replace `if len(args) == 0 { return cmd.Help() }` with usageErr; add rejectUnknownSubcommand RunE on parent commands.
patches.reason: Two related issues: (1) cmd.Help() returns nil so missing positional args silently exited rc=0 in 51 generated commands; (2) parent commands without Run/RunE are non-Runnable in cobra v1.9.1, so `videos garbage` returned flag.ErrHelp (rc=0) before Args validation could run. Fix: make parents Runnable with a RunE that errors on unknown subcommand.
patches.validated_outcome: playlists update --name x (missing <id>) exits rc=2; videos garbage exits rc=3 with 'unknown command'; bare parent (videos with no subcommand) still exits rc=0 with help; valid subcommands still dispatch.

patches.summary: Add long-form --help text clarifying that videos viewed sources from the webhook event stream.
patches.reason: Empty results from videos viewed against a workspace with real views (622 total) was confusing. The rollup is keyed off video.viewed/video.milestone webhook events, not the API counter — empty results don't mean zero views, they mean no view events have hit the inbox yet.
patches.validated_outcome: videos viewed --help now explains the data source and points users to wire a webhook endpoint.

patches.summary: Add UI-matching `remove-buffers`, narrow `trim-edges`, and unofficial-API `find-mistakes` commands and `clips edit-pass` flags. Fix the pre-existing silent no-op in `--trim-silences-gt` (extractSilenceRanges field-shape drift + cut-args shape).
patches.reason: Tella's Cut panel exposes Remove buffers and Find mistakes that the published CLI didn't cover. Live smoke tests against api.tella.com on 2026-05-16 confirmed both POST shapes return 404 — the public OpenAPI has no equivalent. The web UI calls unofficial hosts: www.tella.tv (`/api/stories/{vid}/scenes/{cl}/silences?mode=...` + apply PATCH) for buffer trimming and prod-stream.tella.tv (`/ai-mistakes/analyze-scene` SSE) for mistake detection. Both unofficial hosts reject the public-API Bearer token with 401 `not_authenticated` and require a session cookie issued to a logged-in browser. This patch composes the documented public endpoints (GET /silences + POST /cut) for Remove buffers / Trim edges, and uses a mixed-auth path for Find mistakes (cookie-auth analyze + Bearer-auth public /cut apply) behind a hard `--unofficial` gate. Side-fix: extractSilenceRanges previously only handled `{start,end}`/`{startMs,endMs}` shapes, missing today's `{startTimeMs,durationMs}` response — that's why `--trim-silences-gt N` had been silently planning zero cuts on the live API. The parser now handles both shapes and the planned cut args now use the spec-correct `{fromMs,toMs}`.
patches.validated_outcome: go build/vet/test all clean (24 unit tests across the cli package). Skill verifier: ✓ All checks passed. Live smoke against the test workspace: `videos clips remove-buffers <vid> <cl> --dry-run --min-ms 500` planned 42 cuts from 105 silences; `videos clips trim-edges <vid> <cl> --dry-run` planned head/tail correctly; `videos clips find-mistakes <vid> <cl> --unofficial --detect-only` confirms the unofficial AI service returns detected mistakes when a fresh session cookie is set. Reset via PATCH /clips/{clipId} {cuts:[]} after each mutating run.

deferred_to_upstream.feature: single-POST remove-buffers + find-mistakes endpoints on the public API
deferred_to_upstream.reason: If Tella publishes these on api.tella.com, the per-clip composition wrappers in this patch should be replaced by direct endpoint calls. Tracked at the upstream issue below.
TELLA_SESSION_COOKIE manual paste is brittle. A login flow or browser-extension capture would improve UX; out of scope for this patch.
